paper_title: Synthesis and Evaluation of Chitosan/Diatomite Composite

methods.description: Process for synthesizing a chitosan/diatomite composite material, involving purification of diatomite and combination with chitosan for enhanced material properties.

steps.description: The diatomite powder was subjected to two steps of purification by leaching that were aimed at removing contaminants that had been detected in the raw samples.
steps.searchable_summary: purify diatomite powder by leaching to remove contaminants

steps.description: The first stage entailed the elimination of metallic impurities, which was accomplished by dispersing 5 g of diatomite precursor in diluted hydrochloric acid (10%) for 4 h at 100 °C under continuous mixing employing a magnetic stirrer.
steps.searchable_summary: remove metallic impurities from diatomite using hydrochloric acid

steps.description: Following that, the removal of existing organic contaminants was accomplished by washing the hydrochloric-treated diatomite via H2O2 for approximately 5 h, 3 times.
steps.searchable_summary: remove organic contaminants from diatomite using H2O2

steps.description: To remove any leftover H2O2, we washed the chemically refined diatomite many times with distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: wash diatomite with distilled water to remove H2O2

steps.description: As part of the subsequent processing step, the sample was subjected to an overnight drying process that occurred in an oven at a temperature of 80 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: dry diatomite sample in oven at 80 °C overnight

steps.description: Following that, a homogeneous diatomite slurry was created by dispersing 3 g of the chemically refined diatomite in 50 mL of distilled water and sonicating the mixture (240 W) for 120 min.
steps.searchable_summary: create diatomite slurry by sonicating in distilled water

steps.description: In a separate phase, 3 g of the powdered chitosan was immersed in 50 mL of a solution of acetic acid (0.1 M) and gently introduced to the diatomite mixture.
steps.searchable_summary: immerse chitosan in acetic acid and mix with diatomite

steps.description: For 12 h, the mixture was mixed using sonication vibrations (240 W) and magnetic stirring (800 rpm).
steps.searchable_summary: mix chitosan-diatomite mixture using sonication and stirring

steps.description: After 12 h, the produced composite was completely washed, filtrated to eliminate the remaining acid, and dried overnight at 60 °C for characterization as well as application tests.
steps.searchable_summary: wash, filter, and dry CS/Di composite for characterization

methods.method_name: Comparative Study of Drug Loading and Release
methods.description: Comparison of the loading capacities and release profiles of CS/Di composite as drug carriers with other carriers in the literature.

steps.description: The determined loading capacities and release profiles of CS/Di as potential carriers of both the OXPL and 5-Fu drugs were compared with those of other investigated carriers in the literature (Table 3).
steps.searchable_summary: compare loading capacities and release profiles of CS/Di with other carriers

methods.description: Assessment of the cytotoxicity of CS/Di composite and its drug-loaded forms on colorectal tissue cells and carcinoma cells.

steps.description: The study used freshly derived colorectal tissue cells (CCD-18Co) and cells from colorectal carcinoma (HCT-116) to assess the cytotoxicity of free CS/Di as well as its OXPL and 5-Fu preloaded compounds.
steps.searchable_summary: test cytotoxicity of CS/Di and drug-loaded forms on colorectal cells

steps.description: The measured cell viability percentage of fresh cells after exposure to CS/Di at the highest dose (120 μg/L) is 94.2%.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze cell viability after CS/Di exposure

steps.description: For HCT-116 cells that were treated with CS/Di as separate particulates, the results reveal substantial cytotoxic impacts, especially at evaluated dosages beyond 50 μg/mL.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze cytotoxic impact on HCT-116 cells

steps.description: In comparison to the free OXP and 5-Fu medicines, the loaded formulations show better performance in terms of cytotoxic influences and anticancer activities when applied to the HCT-116 tumor cell lines.
steps.searchable_summary: compare cytotoxic performance of loaded formulations with free drugs
